item_2_question: Lesson Objectives In the early part of the lesson (first ~ 25 minutes) did the tutor clearly state the goals of the lesson before starting the practical part of the lesson (e.g., "Today we will learn…", "Today we will discuss…", "In this lesson we are going to explore…", "By the end of this lesson you will be able to…", "The lesson is about…", "The lesson will be about…", "The lesson is dedicated to…", "Today we will cover…")?
item_2_why: The tutor did not clearly state the lesson objectives at the beginning of the lesson before starting practical activities.
item_2_examples: Maciej Szeląg | Tutor: Dobra. Słuchajcie my już sobie. Możemy. W takim razie powoli przejść do tematu dzisiejszej lekcji. | Maciej Szeląg | Tutor: bo dzisiaj będzie się działo.
item_2_instead: The tutor mentioned moving on to the lesson topic but did not explicitly state the objectives or goals of the lesson.
item_3_question: Revision of the Previous Class Did the tutor ask the students to recall what they studied in the previous class by actively involving them at the beginning or early part of the lesson?
item_3_why: The tutor asked students to recall what they studied in the previous class, and students responded with relevant information.

Właśnie Union, dzila się dobiła.
item_4_question: Homework Review Did the tutor review or ask about the students' home task (even if the word "homework" was not explicitly used), for example by referring to a specific assignment that was meant to be done at home (e.g., "Have you completed your homework assignments?
item_4_why: The tutor reviewed the homework by asking students about their assignments and discussing any issues they faced.

item_20_question: Comment: Question Quality (Quantitative - not scored) What percentage of tutor questions were open-ended vs. closed (yes/no, technical)?
item_20_comment: About 20% open-ended questions, 80% closed/technical questions
item_21_question: Comment: Productive Time Ratio (Quantitative - not scored) Estimate what percentage of the lesson was spent on productive learning activity vs technical/admin/dead time. Return as, for example: "About 82% productive learning time, 18% technical/admin/dead time"
item_21_comment: About 85% productive learning time, 15% technical/admin/dead time
item_22_question: Comment: Cognitive Demand Ratio (Quantitative - not scored) Estimate what percentage of tutor prompts required student thinking/reasoning vs simple procedural compliance. Return as, for example: "About 42% thinking/reasoning prompts, 58% procedural or technical prompts"
item_22_comment: About 40% thinking/reasoning prompts, 60% procedural or technical prompts

item_1_question: Warm-Up and Greetings Did the tutor greet the students in a friendly and engaging manner and explicitly ask at least one small-talk question about the students' week, school, or holidays (e.g., "How was your week?
item_1_why: The tutor greeted the students warmly and engaged in small talk about their backgrounds and personal experiences.
item_1_examples: Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Bom dia. Bom dia. Bom dia. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: E aí, como você está tudo certinho. | Murilo Puga: Eu machuquei minha coxa.
item_2_question: Lesson Objectives In the early part of the lesson (first ~ 25 minutes) did the tutor clearly state the goals of the lesson before starting the practical part of the lesson (e.g., "Today we will learn…", "Today we will discuss…", "In this lesson we are going to explore…", "By the end of this lesson you will be able to…", "The lesson is about…", "The lesson will be about…", "The lesson is dedicated to…", "Today we will cover…")?
item_2_why: The tutor did not clearly state the lesson objectives at the beginning of the lesson. The focus was more on technical setup and platform login.
item_2_examples: Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Já vai colocando aqui a apresentação para a gente iniciar hoje. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Vocês têm uma pequena revisãozinha para fazer lá na plataforma da Cordeland.
item_2_instead: The tutor mentioned starting a new module and a small revision task but did not clearly state the lesson objectives.
item_3_question: Revision of the Previous Class Did the tutor ask the students to recall what they studied in the previous class by actively involving them at the beginning or early part of the lesson?
item_3_why: The tutor asked students to recall what they studied in the previous class, and students responded with relevant information.
item_3_examples: Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Primeiro de tudo, vamos começar revisando o que a gente fez nesse último módulozinho. | Murilo Puga: Salvamos o mundo. | ellen cristina: Procuramos o nosso cenário.
item_4_question: Homework Review Did the tutor review or ask about the students' home task (even if the word "homework" was not explicitly used), for example by referring to a specific assignment that was meant to be done at home (e.g., "Have you completed your homework assignments?
item_4_why: The tutor reviewed the homework by asking if students completed it and discussed the previous assignment.
item_4_examples: Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Isso sobre a liçãozinha de casa. Conseguiram entregar pessoal. | Murilo Puga: Não. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: No final da última aulinhas, nós citamos, né? A liçãozinha de casa de vocês.
item_5_question: Clarity of explanation: Clear and Manageable Instruction Chunking In the middle part of the lesson (from ~ minute 25 to minute ~50) did the tutor break instructions into manageable, child-friendly chunks?
item_5_why: The tutor provided instructions in small, clear steps, allowing students to follow along without overload.
item_5_examples: Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Primeiro de tudo, a gente vai começar com o paredão nessa parede inteira. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: A gente vai abrir a nossa caixa de ferramentas. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Então, ó, vamos iniciando aqui oficialmente agora a nossa M2 L um.
item_6_question: Clarity of Explanation: Effective Modeling Before Expecting Independence In the middle part of the lesson (from ~ minute 25 to minute ~50) did the tutor model the task clearly before expecting students to work independently?
item_6_why: The tutor modeled tasks clearly before expecting students to work independently, demonstrating steps and explaining processes.
item_6_examples: Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Primeiro de tudo, a gente vai começar com o paredão nessa parede inteira. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Eu vou mostrar para vocês um exemplo de parkour que a gente vai estar podendo fazer durante as nossas aulinhas.
item_7_question: Student Engagement (Names) Score 1 only if: - tutor calls at least one student by name more than 2 times during the lesson
item_7_why: The tutor called students by name multiple times throughout the lesson, ensuring personalized engagement.
item_7_examples: Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Bom dia, Heloísa. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Sim, pode falar, Luiza. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: E você, murilo? Tá conseguindo também.
item_8_question: Student Engagement (Participation) Score 1 only if tutor directly invites student participation (answer/read/explain/share idea)
item_8_why: The tutor directly invited student participation by asking them to share their screens, answer questions, and engage in activities.
item_8_examples: Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Quer compartilhar a telinha para a gente ver o que está acontecendo. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Consegue escrever isso na telinha? | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Pode compartilhar a telinha para eu dar uma olhada.
item_9_question: Broad and Inclusive Participation Did the tutor actively involve multiple students rather than relying on one active child?
item_9_why: The tutor actively involved multiple students by using names and inviting participation from different students.
item_9_examples: Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Sim, pode falar, Luiza. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: E você, murilo? Tá conseguindo também. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Quer compartilhar a telinha para a gente ver o que está acontecendo.
item_10_question: Student Engagement (Share Screen / Work) Did the tutor ask students to share their screen OR show their work (e.g., "Share your screen", "Show me what you did", "Please share it with me", "Share your project", "Share your drawing")?
item_10_why: The tutor asked students to share their screens multiple times during the lesson.
item_10_examples: Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Quer compartilhar a telinha para a gente ver o que está acontecendo. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Pode compartilhar a telinha para eu dar uma olhada. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Quer compartilhar a telinha agora, pra ver.
item_11_question: Did the tutor explicitly ask at least one student to turn their camera on (or to keep it on) in order to support attention/participation during the lesson?
item_11_why: There was no explicit request for students to turn on their cameras during the lesson.
item_11_instead: The tutor did not mention cameras or request students to turn them on.
item_12_question: Content delivery: Did the tutor use the platform (e.g., Kodland) or other visual tools to explain assignments or tasks?
item_12_why: The tutor used the platform and visual tools to explain tasks and assignments, guiding students through the process.
item_12_examples: Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: A gente vai abrir a nossa caixa de ferramentas. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Então, ó, vamos iniciando aqui oficialmente agora a nossa M2 L um. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: A gente vai criar um base plate lá daqueles vazios, mesmo para a gente iniciar.
item_13_question: Lesson Wrap-Up (Tutor-led) In the final segment of the lesson (last ~25 minutes), did the tutor deliver a content-based wrap-up by summarizing what was learned or accomplished?
item_13_why: The tutor delivered a content-based wrap-up by summarizing what was learned and accomplished during the lesson.
item_13_examples: Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Então vou mostrar para vocês aqui a reflexão da nossa aulinha de que a gente aprendeu então um pouquinho sobre a criação de obstáculos. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Nós criamos um novo mapinha do zero. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Nós criamos um percurso de parkour, né?
item_14_question: Lesson Wrap-Up (Student-led) In the final segment of the lesson (last ~25 minutes) did the tutor ask students to summarize, reflect on, discuss the lesson, or participate in a revision / summary quiz in the final part of the lesson?
item_14_why: The tutor did not ask students to summarize or reflect on the lesson in the final segment.
item_14_instead: The tutor summarized the lesson content, but students were not invited to reflect or summarize.
item_15_question: Homework for the Next Class (Lesson Structure / Continuity) Score 1 only if tutor clearly describes what students must do at home (task details). Merely mentioning “homework exists” = 0.Clear and Meaningful Next Step
item_15_why: The tutor clearly described the homework task for the next class, providing specific details.
item_15_examples: Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: O que eu tenho para dizer pra vocês é que a liçãozinha de casa de vocês vai ser criar um labirinto. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Então, durante esta semana, vocês terão de desafio aí pela frente. A criação de um labirinto beleza.
item_16_question: Upcoming Lesson (Lesson Structure / Continuity) Score 1 only if tutor clearly states the topic/activity of the next lesson (“Next class we will…”). “See you next week” without topic = 0.
item_16_why: The tutor clearly stated the topic of the next lesson, providing continuity for the students.
item_16_examples: Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Na nossa próxima aulinha. A gente vai aprender mais sobre funções. | Julia Nogueira Figueiredo: Vamos programar aí um obstáculo, tá? Vamos criar uma escada de arco íris e vamos continuar a desenvolver a Tower of Hell.
